leagueBriefing: LSU snapped a cold spell in a big way on Monday night, using a late-inning surge to secure their first SEC win of the season and avoid a series sweep against Texas A&M.

games.headline: LSU bats wake up in series-salvaging win over Texas A&M
games.brief: After struggling to find any rhythm at the plate over the weekend, LSU finally found its spark under the lights at Tiger Park. The Tigers entered the night last in the SEC in batting average, but they looked like a completely different squad on Monday, racking up eight hits and pulling away with a decisive four-run sixth inning. Tatum Clopton provided the stability they needed in the circle, tossing a complete game to help LSU secure a much-needed first conference victory and some momentum heading into their next road trip.
bullets: Tatum Clopton dominated in a complete-game effort, allowing only one earned run while striking out six Aggies.
Alix Franklin provided the early fireworks with a two-out, solo home run in the second inning to give LSU its first lead of the series.
Maci Bergeron blew the game open in the sixth inning with a clutch two-run single that capped off a four-run frame.
The Tigers' eight hits in this contest were more than they produced in the first two games of the series combined.
Texas A&M's Mya Perez was held in check after hitting home runs in each of the first two games of the series.
games.context: The victory marks LSU's first SEC win of the season and avoids a 0-6 start in conference play.
